title: Boeing 737 MAX crisis: public trust collapse after two fatal crashes

ground_truth_outcome.source: Atmosphere Research Group survey, December 2019 — approximately 40% willing to fly MAX

polling_trajectory.source: Morning Consult brand tracking — Boeing net favorability +50 pre-crisis (estimated pro-trust ~78%)
Estimated from Morning Consult — slight decline after Lion Air but not sustained
Reuters/Ipsos poll April 2019: 52% less likely to fly Boeing after crashes
Barclays survey mid-2019: 53% would avoid MAX or pay more for alternative
Estimated from Atmosphere Research Group tracking — willingness plateaued mid-year
Congressional hearings period — internal emails revealed Boeing knew of MCAS issues
End of year: ~40% willing per Atmosphere Research; Muilenburg fired Dec 23

events.description: Lion Air Flight 610 crashes into Java Sea, killing all 189 aboard; MCAS system implicated
Ethiopian Airlines Flight 302 crashes, killing 157; worldwide grounding of 737 MAX follows on March 13
Boeing announces MCAS software fix and pilot training changes; cuts 737 production rate 20%
Paris Air Show: Boeing receives zero new orders for 737 MAX; Airbus dominates with 100+ orders
Congressional hearings: internal Boeing emails reveal employees knew of MCAS problems, mocked FAA oversight
CEO Dennis Muilenburg fired; production halt announced; board installs David Calhoun as replacement

agents.name: Dennis Muilenburg
agents.description: Boeing CEO (fired Dec 2019), defended company throughout

agents.name: Peter DeFazio
agents.description: Chair, House Transportation Committee — led investigation

agents.name: Ralph Nader
agents.description: Consumer advocate whose grandniece died in Ethiopian crash

agents.name: FAA
agents.description: Aviation regulator — criticized for delegating certification to Boeing

agents.name: EASA (European regulator)
agents.description: Demanded independent review, refused to follow FAA lead

agents.name: Airlines (Southwest, American, United)
agents.description: Major MAX operators — publicly supported Boeing while seeking compensation

agents.name: Frequent flyers
agents.description: Business travelers — more informed, initially trusted Boeing

agents.name: General flying public
agents.description: Casual flyers — reactive to media coverage of crashes

agents.name: Victims' families and advocates
agents.description: Families of 346 crash victims — powerful media presence

notes: Pro = trust Boeing / willing to fly 737 MAX, Against = distrust / would refuse to fly. This is an asymmetric crisis: trust starts very high and erodes under repeated shocks. Polling data for rounds 1-2 and 5-6 are estimates based on known survey ranges; rounds 3-4 have direct survey citations. Boeing stock dropped from ~$370 (pre-Ethiopian crash) to ~$325 (year-end 2019) despite broader S&P 500 rising 29%.
